title: Mountains
description: Prominent mountains and high places that frame journeys, revelation, and worship.

features.name: Mount Sinai / Horeb
features.description: The mountain of covenant, law, and prophetic encounter.
features.caution: The precise mountain identification is debated, so the marker is intentionally cautious.

features.name: Mount Carmel
features.description: A coastal ridge famous for Elijah's confrontation with the prophets of Baal.
features.caution: The ridge is secure, though the narrative includes a broader contest area on the slopes.

features.name: Mount Zion
features.description: The hill associated with Jerusalem's sacred and royal center.
features.caution: The term can refer to the hill, the city, or the broader theological symbol.

features.name: Mount of Olives
features.description: The ridge east of Jerusalem associated with prayer, teaching, and the Passion Week.
features.caution: The location is clear, though individual event spots on the ridge are reconstructed.

features.name: Mount Hermon
features.description: A high northern mountain that marks the upper end of the land's topography.
features.caution: The mountain is secure, but the symbolic associations are broader than the summit itself.
